texto: [Hilary] ...after you left, I didn't understand what had happened. David, I don't hate you and I don't blame you. I don't think you were happy, and I wasn't that happy either. We were just coasting, seeing what would happen, and then you pulled the plug. Right?
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Janice Y.K. Lee que provoca limites e escolhas conscientes. Ao questionar “[Hilary] ...after you left, I didn't understand what had happened. David, I don't hate you and I …”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre reflexao, blame, conciliation, com foco em conciliation e divorce.

frase_original: [Hilary] ...after you left, I didn't understand what had happened. David, I don't hate you and I don't blame you. I don't think you were happy, and I wasn't that happy either. We were just coasting, seeing what would happen, and then you pulled the plug. Right?

texto: [Margaret] went to a talk on parenting at the end of the school year where the speaker had said that doing good things, charitable things, was actually a selfish act, because it made you feel good. She has been mulling that ever since. Should she do something selfless, something good? Should she reach out to someone who really needs her forgiveness? Would this make her feel better?
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Janice Y.K. Lee que provoca temas ligados a educacao.
